no you fucked it up - you need to explain exactly wtf the plan is - the original thing you did was amazing, idk why you changed all of it instead of just the risk parts i mentioned.  - 

Background Agents



Different from subagents - Subagents have their own "profiles" - A background agent is one time fire and forget thing that has no related files created for it.



How a background agent should work - Exactly like claude codes background agent system.



So - Say im in the middle of programming - and i tell the ai something about its memory, maybe it did something i ddint like and i told it not to do that, and then in the same prompt also asked it to then proceed with the code changed for whatever program.



The current flow is - the ai will proceed to update its memory - and then proceeded to to implement the code.



Now this works absolutely beautifully right now as is - so i want to make sure that we are only adding this as a feature and not modifying the original flow (too much atleast)



Basically the goal for that example would be to- 



**My prompt abt memory update and code changes*



AI Spawns Background agent
  {Background agent spawn: Update XXX memory file with XXX - User stated "Xxx"[Start]
*AI Proceeds to make code edits and does not worry about updating the memory*
{Background Agent to main chat AI: "Done - I have updated the memory - now says (XXXX)"
AI proceeds to get the background agents response after it completes the coding tasks automatically - or we create a {Background agent progress} tool. - that returns {in progress or Agent Result:}
Ai Sends final response in chat session with both memory updated and code updated.


Now - this background agent - 



It should be a second LLM/API call - so its essentially like me sending a second prompt. 



 It should be the same exact main chat agent, nothing different from it - the same thing. - the only thing is - instead of being triggered and acting upon my prompt - irs acting upon the prompt given during the background agent spawn tool


This could be a very powerful tool - for anything



Imagine a news team with multiple subagents  - instead of having a single ai that goes to multiple sites all in one call searching for news, the Subagent can spawn multiple background agents, each with a different news site to scrape/visit



That way it reduces the amount of work a single agent is doing while still being efficient - and yes im aware that sometimes this may cause (in the news example) potentially an extra 5 AI calls in one pass for 5 different background agents searching 5 different news sites - but its efficient and should work.
